steps.title: Use the creativity clue
steps.content: The passage says readers may think Celine merely fictionalized true events. Since creativity and inventiveness are prized in this field, that perception would make the novel seem less creatively impressive than it really is.

steps.title: Reject unrelated drawbacks
steps.content: Choice B shifts to identifying real-world counterparts, which is not the concern. Choice C invents a claim about what Celine should have said. Choice D introduces disagreement among critics that the passage never mentions. The logical drawback is that the novel's imaginative achievement could be underestimated. The answer is A.
